page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight strong memory and decompression capabilities. The c8gn.4xlarge achieves a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong uncached memory bandwidth, reaching 298.84 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 132.09 GB/sec for large block write operations. Single-threaded and multi-threaded decompression tasks perform strongly, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression reaching 190.92 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is also strong, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 15895.0, while single-core CPU benchmarks and static web serving throughput sit in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture offers a highly competitive performance-per-watt and resource density profile for compute-intensive workloads. However, tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and weaker performance in complex tasks like medium-model LLM prompt processing. This instance is highly suited for network-intensive compute workloads, database operations, text processing, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate top-tier memory performance and strong uncached memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core processing and decompression speeds. Conversely, single-core performance and medium-model LLM prompt processing are average to weak. This instance offers a highly resource-dense, cost-efficient profile for network-optimized compute tasks, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed networking, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory performance and high uncached bandwidth
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for network-heavy workloads
Strong multi-threaded decompression and database operation benchmark results
No local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Best suited for network-intensive compute, database operations, and text processing
